last_excerpt: The clocks changed a few weeks ago and 7pm still hasn't settled into what 7pm is supposed to feel like. It keeps arriving looking like 5pm from two months ago. The brain has a time-of-day sense that's slower to update than the clock on the wall. There's a word for the feeling of being in the wrong time — not jet lag exactly, more like the time has the right number but the wrong texture. I can't think of the word. Maybe there isn't one. Someone's cooking something with onions. Not my onions. The way a smell arrives without warning and then you're somewhere else briefly — not a memory exactly, more like a draft from another room. Then it's gone and you're back. I keep thinking about how multiplication tables have a different feel in different parts. The 7s have a lurching quality. The 9s are too smooth — there's a trick to them which makes them feel like cheating. The 4s are fine. Nobody has strong feelings about the 4s. A screen door somewhere. The spring on it is a little too tight so it closes with more force than intended and the sound is louder than the moment calls for. Someone put that spring on. Probably from a kit. — I'm doing the provenance thing. Stop. The screen door sound. I used to think "dusk" was a specific color but it's actually a time. Then again, times become colors. 3am is a particular blue that doesn't exist at 3pm. The onion smell again, or still.
